title: oxalis ricardii
intro: Oxalis ricardii, also known as the Mexican sorrel or wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting kidney function. Oxalis ricardii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
